I was in XXXX XXXX, XXXX at a bar on XX/XX/21 with a friend, and when I attempted to pay the bill for drinks, I was told we owed {$8000.00} XXXX XXXX. I handed my XXXX credit card I use for daily purchases to the waiter who walked away to charge the card, but when he returned, I was told the card declined. I then proceeded to attempt to pay with another XXXX card and I was told it declined too. The same happened with a XXXX XXXX I tried to use. I then tried to pay with my Barclays card, but again the waiter returned to say the card declined, and I would need to pay with cash. A representative of the bar then took me to a nearby ATM, and I made a withdrawal, and paid my bill in cash to pay this. I was given no receipt, but I was allowed to leave the bar at this point. It was not until I checked my Barclay account online much later that I discovered the Barclays card was charged! Not only was I lied to about the transaction, but the card should NOT have been charged at all because I paid in cash, and the amount also charged is outrageous, and not in line with my bill that day. Plus, I never signed a receipt for that charge. When I noticed the charge, I contacted Barclays to help resolve the matter because I had no way to contact the bar, and I do not speak good enough Spanish to have been able to discuss the matter with them either way. I provided a copy to Barclays of the ATM transaction for the cash that I used to pay the merchant, as well as the text messages from both XXXX XXXX & XXXX showing the merchant was trying to charge my account random and outrageous amounts which were not authorized by me and are clearly fraudulent. text: I made a puchase at XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX for {$52.00}. On XX/XX/XXXX, prior to the closing date of the billing cycle, I received a charge from XXXX for {$1.00}, which was accidentally charged twice, for a bounce back of my checking account. I had gotten divorced and opened a new checking account at the same bank because the bank was unable to remove either spouse from the joint checking account, but I don't use the XXXX XXXX card very often, and didn't realize that I hadn't updated the account number. I called XXXX and chatted with them online several times on XX/XX/XXXX, and they credited back the fee when I explained that this was an honest misunderstanding, I have never been late, and I have excellent credit ( score around XXXX ), and held the credit card account for over 10 years. I spoke to XXXX different people who said the account was now cleared, and I got a confirmation that the total of {$52.00} was paid. Nobody had mentioned that the automatic bill pay feature was XXXX, or that I needed to make a one-time manual payment. On XX/XX/XXXX, I receive a notification from several credit card monitoring services that my credit score dropped over XXXX points!! I have always had an almost-perfect credit score, always have automatic bill pay, and have never been late. I called XXXX to inquire what had happened, and was informed that the payment of {$52.00} did not process on their end. I immediately paid the total on the app while I was on the phone with the agent, and asked them to clear this late payment from my credit reports, as this was all a misunderstading, and I had already called on XX/XX/XXXX to reverse the charge, update the checking account, and had received an update that the payment had gone through. I was told by the agent that they can not update the credit reporting agencies, and that I will just have to accept an XXXX hit to my almost-perfect credit score that I have spent decades meticulously monitoring and cultivating. If you view my credit report, you will see that I have an installment mortage account, credit cards with a long history ( oldest account was opened in XXXX - XXXX ), and I have never had a late payment and pay all of my accounts off in full every month without fail ( except the mortgage - I cant afford to pay the entire mortgage off in one month, or I wouldnt need a mortgage! ). Please help. I have already contacted Kohls ' several times, and have paid the amount in full the MOMENT I found out there was a discrepancy. This is not equitable to suffer such a large blow to my credit score all because they didn't process the payment when I updated my checking account, after receiving a notification that the payment was processing. I am the farthest thing from a credit risk.

I did not get a response to this email, either. Soon after this, I filed a dispute with Citi Bank, case number XXXX. When I filed this dispute, the customer service agent told me that I was instantly going to receive a conditional credit to my account for the amount in dispute of {$140.00}. A few days later, I had checked my checking account and noticed that I did not receive the credit to my account. I called Citi Bank and they said that they do not offer conditional credit in these cases ; the previous customer service agent was uninformed, or lied. I said okay and let them process the case. On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a paper in the mail asking for more information about my case. I filled the paper out, stating all of the details about my dispute and sent the paper to the email address provided on XX/XX/2023. On XXXX XXXX I spoke to Citi Customer support to see what was the status on my case. The agent told me that my case was denied because I did not provide enough information. She said however, I can appeal the case at my nearest Citi Bank branch. She said to print the emails I sent to the merchant, as well as any proof of my purchase. I printed the XXXX emails I sent to the merchant, and the invoice that the merchant sent me. When I arrived at the branch they told me that theyre not sure why support sent me to the branch, as they have to do this over the phone with customer support. text: PERSONAL ACCT FRAUD COMPLAINT I was a victim of unauthorized wire fraud. The fraud on my personal account was the second of XXXX wire transfers initiated on XX/XX/22. The first was made from my Chase Business account. This one made from my personal checking account, since it was after XXXX, could not be completed until the following day. The criminal convinced me not to sign into my accounts after we got off the phone while the " Federal Reserve XXXX '' monitored my account for the next XXXX hours with the hope that XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX in again and they could collect his IP address. This was a criminal offense, he explained, and they would bring criminal charges if they could catch him. At the end of the call, he told me he would call me back in the morning to follow up and to wait for his call. In my non-critical state of mind due to illnessI had been sleeping all day and was awakened by the call at XXXX believed the criminal was trying to help me. I went back to sleep after the call until the next morning. DETAILS OF CALL The criminal, posing as a Chase representative by the name XXXX, convinced me he was legitimate because the number he was calling from matched the number on my Chase Business Debit card, XXXX. He had a lot of knowledge about my personal and business accounts. At no time before, during, or after this encounter did I ever share any account numbers, social security numbers, addresses, or phone numbers with him or anyone. He explained with some urgency that he needed to help me reverse several fraudulent wire transfers that were currently in progress by XXXX XXXX of XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX. Amounts he initially mentioned : {$7200.00}, {$5000.00}, {$1800.00}, aligned with the amounts in the XXXX accounts he was referring to, respectively my personal checking, my business checking, and my business savings accounts. At every curious point, he satisfied my doubts and questions and stepped me through, screen by screen, what he explained was the reversal of these in-progress wire transfers. It dawned on me too latethe following afternoonthat he had duped me into unwittingly participating in the actual XXXX wire transfers that would deplete my personal and business accounts. I am shocked and mortified that I was tricked by this criminal. He reached me at just the right moment, having just awakened at XXXX having slept all day due to illness. I have appealed to JP Morgan Chase Bank, a company Ive been a customer with for decades, to do everything in its power to recall the transfers made to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX account. But my bank seems to have a loophole in protecting me since I unwittingly became a participant in this fraud. I am a single, middle income XXXX, and this criminal depleted my accounts of all my liquid funds.

text: On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased a {$1000.00} money order from USPS. I did not need it so I deposited into my CHASE account VIA ATM machine. On XXXX it cleared ( or so I thought ) and for the entire day my account was in positive status. At XXXX I get an email stating my account was - {$270.00}. They wiped out all of my money in my account. When I contacted them they said it wasn't THEIR fault, it was the fault of the USPS, that REFUSED payment. When I called USPS they informed me that the money order was NEVER presented for payment, and wherever it is its still a viable MO. My theory is that CHASE presented the MO with the wrong serial number or did something in ERROR and I am paying for it as the customer. They are closing my account, and USPS has told me that since they wont give the MO back ( only provides a legal copy ) they would have to do a 60 day inquiry in order to see why this happened before refunding my money. I have been to CHASE branches, where the employees are more than helpful, but cant do anything because " the return process has started. '' I am in a hellish nightmare, cant eat cant sleep had to stop my direct deposit, borrow money, and even facing eviction, and can't believe this is happening to me. The worse part is them saying USPS refused payment.I feel I was profiled for depositing the MO in the ATM. CHASE felt that a XXXX year old grandmother with the zip code in an urban area shouldn't have {$1000.00} and something must be fishy, so just give it back. They won't admit they're wrong, won't help me, has spoken to me horribly on the phone, BRANDED ME A CRIMINAL, all this while me and my family suffer. I am BEYOND devastated by this. Even the employees I have spoken to by going to the branches have tried their best to help me by scanning their fraud department my receipts making calls to help and they are turned away. THEY have even said THIS IS WRONG. I am at my wits end with this institution unwilling to admit their mistake and PROFILING me ... PLEASE HELP ME

text: I opened a Chase savings account on XX/XX/20 and deposited a {$2500.00} and {$5000.00} check.
